title: With Al infrastructure spending exploding (Cisco +15%), how are you positioning in your trade?
author: Safe-Display8632
selftext:
everyone already talked about Cisco last a few days ago. i was late so I have to be quick if i wanted to catch a move. so I headed to Bitget's getclaw and here's my findings.  
  
i completely missed the 15% earnings move in real time. Got annoyed at myself, then actually decided to dig into the earnings properly instead of just doom-scrolling headlines. Ran some of the numbers through GetClaw too and honestly... i can see why the market reacted this hard.  
  
The bull case is definitely there:  
\- AI infrastructure orders guidance got raised from $5.3B to $9B  
\- Networking revenue grew 25%  
\- Data center switching orders up 40%  
\- Revenue guidance for next year is now the highest in company history  
  
The market basically stopped looking at Cisco as a boring old networking company and started pricing it as an AI infrastructure play.  
  
But I still think people are ignoring a few things:  
\- Gross margins are getting weaker  
\- Some legacy business segments are still soft  
\- Insiders were selling shares before earnings  
\- The stock already ran 25%+ in basically a week and is sitting near highs now  
  
imo, the rerating already happened. That was probably the easiest money.  
  
Short term im mildly bearish unless Nasdaq keeps pushing higher and carries everything with it. Feels like everyone suddenly became bullish on the exact same AI story at the same time  
My bigger concern long term is how concentrated the demand is.a few hyperscalers slowing spending could change this story very fast.  
  
so genuinely asking, for people still bullish here at these prices... what’s the actual thesis now beyond just “AI infrastructure”? Do you think getclaw and i are going the right way?
